revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳| 名称=神門5号墳| 所在地=[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]高柳字塚ノ越| 規模=| 築造年代=5世紀中頃| 出土品=埴輪|}} ''...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=神門5号墳| 所在地=[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]高柳字塚ノ越| 規模=| 築造年代=5世紀中頃| 出土品=埴輪|}} '''高柳銚子塚古墳'''（たかやなぎちょうしづかふん）は、[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]にある[[前方後円墳]]である。
==概要== [[金鈴塚古墳]]など後期首長系古墳の群在する浜長須賀古墳群の北方1.8キロの砂丘状に単独で立地する。主軸を南北に向ける前方後円墳であったが、明治末年の鉄道工事の際に前方部が破壊され、戦時中に陸軍高射砲指揮所の設置により後円部中央が大きくえぐられた。墳丘から埴輪が採取されており、5世紀中頃の築造とみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 大塚初重・小林三郎・熊野正也 「日本古墳大辞典」p366 東京堂出版 1989年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧#千葉県]]
{{デフォルトソート:こうとここうふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:木更津市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

==概要== [[金鈴塚古墳]]など後期首長系古墳の群在する浜長須賀古墳群の北方1.8キロの砂丘状に単独で立地する。主軸を南北に向ける前方後円墳であったが、明治末年の鉄道工事の際に前方部が破壊され、戦時中に陸軍高射砲指揮所の設置により後円部中央が大きくえぐられた。墳丘から埴輪が採取されており、5世紀中頃の築造とみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 大塚初重・小林三郎・熊野正也 「日本古墳大辞典」p366 東京堂出版 1989年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧#千葉県]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kisarazu]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |Coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |Construction Period = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} revision.comment: more mass replacements to get the template working
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |Alternate name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu, Chiba|ja|木更津市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=draft}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=draft}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=draft}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=draft}}
