Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}}

However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author=[[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]]|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}}

{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17209455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author=[[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]]|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17209455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author=[[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]]|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. {{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author=[[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]]|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba|1=Kisarazu}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. {{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun |image = {{No image available|cat=Kisarazu City}} |alternate_name = Nagasuzuka Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba|1=Kisarazu}}, Chiba Prefecture, Takayanagi, Tsukanoe |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402889|N|139.929139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |area = Uncertain (over 110 meters?){{Cite web|和書|author={{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|1=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum}}|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kisarazu.html|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun: Kisarazu City Kofun (Lower Obitsugawa Left Bank)|work=Shibayama Town|accessdate=2020-11-15}} |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Takayanagi Chōshizuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=10.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=55|lon_sec=44.9|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|10.4|N|139|55|44.9|E}}}} }} '''Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun''' (高柳銚子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba|1=Kisarazu}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on a sand dune 1.8 kilometers north of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which includes late-period chief kofun such as {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kane no Suzu Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kane no Suzu Kofun|qq=}}. Its main axis aligns northeast-southwest, and it was originally over 110 meters long, making it the third-largest kofun in Chiba Prefecture. However, during the late Meiji period, railway construction destroyed the front section. Additionally, the rear section was heavily damaged during World War II due to the establishment of an army anti-aircraft gun command post. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments recovered from the tumulus suggest its construction dates back to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. File:高柳銚子塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|Haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|ja|木更津市郷土博物館金のすず|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu Local History Museum Kane no Suzu|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *{{Cite book|和書|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=1989|page=366|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#千葉県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|version=101919844|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高柳銚子塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17209455}} [[Category:Image requests in Kisarazu City]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
